title: gladiolus calcaratus
intro: Gladiolus calcaratus, also known as the 'rock gladiolus', is a perennial plant species in the family Iridaceae, characterized by its corms and showy flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, where it is believed to possess medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Gladiolus calcaratus include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although the effectiveness and safety of these preparations require further scientific validation.
